item.title: Gears of War: E-Day will emerge on 6th October and will be an Xbox console exclusive
item.content:encoded: Gears of War: E-Day will emerge on 6th October and will be an Xbox console exclusive - Stefan L
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Announced at Xbox Showcase 2026
item.content:encoded:
The summer games mess of announcements continues with the Xbox Games Showcase today with Microsoft showcasing titles from their studios, and others. It marks the first time we'll see Asha Sharmin headlining one of these events, along with Matt Booty as the head of Xbox Games Studios. They'll be debuting after Geoff Keighley took stage on Friday with a lot of reveals, and PlayStation was earlier in the week.

Note: This is a live updated recap of everything announced during the event. If an announcement isn't shown here yet, you may try refreshing and it will be there or will be soon. Additional information and trailers may be added at the end or as time permits. All of it is is in essentially chronological order.

Opening with a thanks for a great 25 years, the message then said here's to another great 25 as Xbox is celebrating its 25th anniversary this year. 

Gears of War E-Day Release Date and Xbox Exclusivity

A trailer then debuts of a Civil Emergency Message for the coalition, as Gears of War: E-Day kicks off the top of the show, just like its promised to have additional coverage after the broadcast. We see the aliens invading Earth, and wandering the streets our protagonists have guns and are trying to figure out what is going on as a modern city is being torn apart with cars tossed, creatures running about, and roads being torn up.

Cover shooting is the order of the day, as you would expect for Gears of War, and we see several different weapons before cutting ahead to someone in armor wielding a machine gun who meets our protagonists in civilians clothes. Mantling, active reload systems, and squad focus gameplay, as well as visceral melee options from bayonets and knives all appear. Gears of Wars: E-Day will release on October 6th, 2026 and will be an Xbox console exclusive - not releasing on PlayStation 5 or Nintendo Switch 2.

Fable Villain Revealed

We see Playgrounds Games up next, with their upcoming, and recently delayed Fable, with a cinematic trailer hosted by Isabel, Hero of Westmirch. We see a scattering of various things from past Fable games including the talking doors, characters reacting to things, and lots of adventuring. Isabel tells us Albion is broken, and it doesn't need 'heroes' but rather 'a hero' and that hero is her as she appears to be the villain of your journey. It will be released on my brithday February 23rd, 2027.

Halo Campaign Evolved Release Date Announced

Halo Campaign Evolved showcased more shooting action, and we got to see some of the new missions that will be released with this Halo remake. The 3 missions take place a year before the ring and showcase something different from Master Chief. Halo: Campaign Evolved releases on July 28th, 2026 and will be on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Resonance A Plague Tale Legacy Announced

A new Plague Tale was revealed by Asobo and features adventuring, exploring and lots of terrible things going on in the world. Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy release date is August 27th, 2026 on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Persona 4 Revival Announced

After a number of rumors and leaks, a remake of Persona 4 was revealed titled PErsona 4 Revival. We also got to see a Persona 4: Revival release date of February 18th, 2027 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. It will be on game pass as well.

State of Decay 3 Coming Next Year

More zombie and horror action are coming with State of Decay 3 is to release in 2027 with all new features and visuals for the series.

Sea of Thieves Custom Content Coming

Are you a modder or like user generated content? Sea of Thieves is adding 'custom seas' which will allow a lot of custom modes, islands and other unique ideas. It will be available on June 18th as part of Season 20.

Metro 2039 Transits in February

Announced as proudly made in Ukraine, we see a desolete area with a lot of ruined area, and high detailed guns and dark areas as well as gas masks and explosives, that fit a certain genre of games. References to a metro, quickly identifies this is a new Metro game from 4A Games as things have continued to get worse in Metro 2039. Metro 2039 is releasing in February for PC, Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/everything-announced-at-xbox-showcase-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Confirms Gears Of War: E-Day Will Stay Exclusive And Not Come To PlayStation 5
item.content:encoded:
New Xbox CEO Asha Sharma said the thing console loyalists have been begging for: Gears of War: E-Day will remain an exclusive. Despite reported plans to previously bring the upcoming shooter to PlayStation 5, Microsoft has seemingly reversed course in an attempt to win back hardcore fans.

The surprise exclusivity was Sharma’s first announcement of the Xbox summer showcase, arriving after several minutes of gameplay for the upcoming Gears prequel was revealed in an extended trailer. The change-up comes after a Gears of War: E-Day placeholder page on Walmart for a PS5 version appeared online last night before later being deleted.

Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announces Gears E-Day will go back to being an Xbox Series X/S console exclusive pic.twitter.com/Hlji1fZUlW

— AmericanTruckSongs9 (@ethangach) June 7, 2026

The question of exclusivity has been the biggest one facing Sharma since she took over the top job from Phil Spencer earlier this year. While Microsoft appeared to be shifting to being primarily a third-party publisher and bringing all of its major games to other platforms, Sharma’s first 100 days have been marked by the drum beat of “returning to Xbox.” Part of that reset now appears to be taking a retreat from putting everything on PS5.

“I think it’s a tough topic,” Sharma said in an interview last week. “Look, we’re the number-two publisher in the world. And in order to be a great publisher, you must have your games reach large audiences to play.”

But she continued, “At the same time, we’re increasingly becoming a platform. In order to be a platform, you must have exclusive content and services. And so we’re looking at that very closely. I think that we have to be very thoughtful about each title and how we want to think about it and learn from similar cases in the industry.”

That doesn’t mean everything at Xbox will now be exclusive. Forza Horizon 6 is still seemingly coming to PS5 later this year, and Fable has already been announced as a multiplatform release as well. But this latest move does signal that other first-party games can no longer be assumed to be coming to anything beyond Xbox and PC at launch.



https://kotaku.com/xbox-confirms-gears-of-war-e-day-will-stay-exclusive-and-not-come-to-playstation-5-2000703617
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears Of War E-Day Gameplay Shows Off The Chaotic Moment Locust Arrived
item.content:encoded:
Xbox kicked off its big Xbox Summer Game Fest Showcase with an extended look at gameplay for Gears of War: E-Day, the next mainline game in the cover shooter series. It’s out this October exclusively on Xbox and PC.

In the new gameplay footage, we see Marcus and Dom fighting through city streets and store interiors against the Locust as they first began their attack on the planet. During the fighting, we see Gears staples, like active reloads and retro lancers.

Later, the gameplay shows some very classic Gears of War combat, including Marcus fighting off iconic enemies in his old Gears suit. Or I guess it’s new here as it’s a prequel.

Interestingly, after the trailer, new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma appeared on camera to confirm that Gears of War will be a console exclusive, despite previous leaks indicating a PS5 port. Those seem to be wrong, or perhaps suggest that Xbox has changed course.

Gears of War: E-Day is set to arrive on Xbox Series X/S and PC and nothing else on October 6.



https://kotaku.com/gears-of-war-e-day-gameplay-shows-off-the-chaotic-day-locust-arrived-2000703612
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Persona 4 Revival Release Date Revealed During XBOX Games Showcase 2026
item.content:encoded:
Persona 4 Revival has been given an official release date of February 18, 2027, during the XBOX Games Showcase 2026.

The highly anticipated remake of Persona 4 was announced at last year’s event, though details surrounding the return of Teddie and co. have been kept under close wraps—until now.

More Persona 4 Revival Details

As you can imagine, Persona 4 Revival might be a from-the-ground-up remake, but it hasn’t lost any of the original’s sauce. The new trailer showcased plenty of the og settings, and each one looks as visually striking as before, with a significant bump bringing the game in line with Persona 5.

If you’re a relatively new fan to the Persona franchise, and you’ve only played Persona 5, then Persona 4 Revival is looking to be the perfect entry point for one of the most beloved games in the series to date—and it should tide you over until Persona 6 eventually rolls around.

Check out the brand new trailer for yourself below:

This was just one of the many announcements during the XBOX Games Showcase. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

What do you make of Persona 4 Revival? If you played the original or remaster, will you be jumping back in? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra not arriving in 2026, as well as everything that was announced during Summer Game Fest.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/persona-4-revival-release-date-revealed-during-xbox-games-showcase-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
During the latest iteration of the XBOX Games Showcase, the developers finally announced the early access and release dates for Halo Campaign Evolved, with the title set to arrive in July 2026.

The last time players received new details about Halo Campaign Evolved was in October 2025, when the developers released a 13-minute gameplay demo and announced the PS5 release. Now, the day has finally come to let players know when they can dive into this remake.

Halo Campaign Evolved Release Date Set for July 2026

During the latest showcase event, Halo Campaign Evolved received another full-blown trailer, and at the end, players were informed of the release and early access dates. The game is set to release on July 28, 2026. The announcement also informed players that It will be available Day One with Game Pass.

The upcoming title is a modernized remake of Halo: Combat Evolved’s campaign, allowing players to experience the original story with high-definition visuals, updated cinematics, and refined controls, along with three new prequel missions featuring the Master Chief and Sgt. Johnson. The campaign is available to play solo or in a 4-player online co-op mode, featuring full crossplay and cross-progression support. This makes it an excellent option for long-time fans returning to the series, while also serving as an inviting entry point for new players, particularly those on PlayStation.

In other news, a new report details the next mainline campaign project from Halo Studios and includes a job listing. Additionally, Halo Studios is apparently hard at work trying to deliver the original trilogy all over again, with Halo 1, Halo 2, and Halo 3 Remakes. What are your thoughts on Halo Campaign Evolved’s release date? Will you be playing it on XBOX or PS5? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Stuntman Hollywood not being allowed to show Jurassic Park in the reveal trailer. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/halo-campaign-evolved-release-date-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The Coalition and XBOX Game Studios revealed the October release date for Gears of War E-Day at the XBOX Games Showcase 2026.

Regarding the release of Gears of War: E-Day, there has been considerable confusion about whether the game would be available on PlayStation 5. According to the latest announcement, it appears that a PS5 release is not on the cards as it is an XBOX console exclusive. However, the game will launch soon on Xbox Series X/S and PC.

Gears of War: E-Day Releases on (insert date)

The Coalition provided players with a brand-new gameplay trailer for Gears of War: E-Day at the XBOX Games Showcase. Towards the end, they announced the release date for the upcoming title on October 6, 2026. Gears E-Day pre-orders go live today, right after the showcase. The title will also be available Day One on Game Pass

The trailer showcased extensive gameplay, featuring Marcus fighting numerous monsters, armed only with makeshift weapons he found along the way and without any protective gear. The city lay in ruins, with derailed trains scattered around and a horde of monsters emerging from the ground, some wielding their own weapons. Additionally, we saw a massive claw-like figure erupting from the earth.

About Gears of War E-Day

For those unaware, the reason there’s so much hype for the upcoming installment, Gears of War: E-Day, is that it arrives as a prequel, set 14 years before Gears of War. Popular characters Marcus Fenix and Dom Santiago make an appearance again, this time returning home to face a new threat: the Locust Horde. The Locust Horde is a subterranean monster that is laying siege to humanity itself.

In other news, here’s everything that was announced at the latest iteration of XBOX Games Showcase. Additionally, the Gears of War Netflix movie adaptation has received a promising update from the director. Will you be playing Gears of War E-Day, when it releases on October 6? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG no longer moving forward. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/gears-of-war-e-day-release-date-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Fable Footage Reveals Its Villain, Isabel Hero Of Wraithmarsh, Played By Hayley Atwell
item.content:encoded:
The latest trailer for Fable, the February 2027 sequel from Playground Game (which was recently delayed), showcased a new character with Isabel Hero of Wraithmarsh. Isabel is performed Hayley Atwell (Captain America: The First Avenger) as confirmed on the Xbox Blog, where it shares more actor announcements for the game.

Isabel claims to be a hero – the one hero Albion needs – but there is an undercurrent of evil to her monologue. “Isabel is a driven, powerful Hero on a quest to make right a tragic injustice," narrative director Craig Owens writes. "Driven by grief, Isabel has turned her pain into determination and focus that sets her at odds with The Hero of Briar Hill and Humphry, her one-time guardian. Isabel’s conviction that she’s not just doing the right thing, but she’ll be able to make right any harm she causes along the way makes her a powerful, dangerous enemy.”

Over on the Xbox Blog, Playground Games also shared that What We Do In The Shadows' Natasia Demetriou will also be in the game as Jenny. “Brutal, crude, and shameless, Jenny is one of the two feuding heirs to the city of Bloodstone," Owens writes. "She embodies Bloodstone as it is – proud, defiant, and dangerous. And, if she gets her way, how it always will be.”

The trailer also ends with what appears to be the return of Jack of Blades, the antagonist from the original Fable. Jack of Blades, however, has always worn a mask, so it could just be someone taking on the mantle.

Fable will launch on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC sometime in February of next year. 



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/new-fable-footage-reveals-its-villain-isabel-hero-of-wraithmarsh
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears Of War: E-Day Launches As A Console Exclusive On Xbox Series X/S And PC This October
item.content:encoded:
Today's Xbox Games Showcase brought our first look at gameplay for Gears of War: E-Day in a new trailer featuring Marcus Fenix wearing... very normal clothes?? That's likely because E-Day is about Emergency Day, or the day in the Gears of War universe where the Locust made themselves known from their underground hives. 

Marcus, answering the call to protect the city he seemingly lives in, picks up a gun and gets to work, and it looks awesome, showcasing a key piece of Gears of War's history we've never seen in a game. Plus, this gameplay trailer revealed that Gears of War: E-Day launches exclusively on October 6 on Xbox Series X/S and PC (despite rumors of a PlayStation 5 release).

Check it out in the Gears of War: E-Day gameplay reveal trailer below: 

As you can see, Gears of War: E-Day still retains the classic third-person cover shooter DNA of the prior Gears of War games, though this one is decidedly even more bloody and violent. Its visual fidelity is impressive, too, with developer The Coalition showcasing its Unreal Engine 5 prowess. 

In case this is your first time hearing about the game, Gears of War: E-Day is an origin prequel for the series, set 14 years before the events of the first Gears of War game, and as the name implies, it chronicles the events of longtime protagonist Marcus Fenix as he navigates the eruption of the Locust Horde's siege on humanity. 

Gears of War: E-Day launches October 6, 2026, as a console exclusive game on Xbox Series X/S and PC. 



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/gears-of-war-e-day-launches-as-a-console-exclusive-on-xbox-series-xs
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved arrives in July, and it's still coming to PS5 despite Xbox shake-ups
item.content:encoded:
One of the easiest guesses you could've made going into the 2026 summer showcase season was that Xbox would announce the release date for Halo: Campaign Evolved. The game, which had been rumoured for about a year, was only officially unveiled last October.

The moment has now arrived, as it was just announced that Halo: Campaign Evolved will be in our hands 23rd July, 2026. The game will launch on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC (via Steam and Xbox). This is actually quite surprising, because Xbox kicked off the showcase by announcing Gears of War: E-Day is now an Xbox console exclusive, reneging on its multi-plat startegy.

The game is also getting new missions. "Before the events of Halo: Combat Evolved, the Master Chief and Sgt Johnson take part in Operation: Meteorite, in which they infiltrate a mysterious Covenant ship on a clandestine assignment," reads a blurb for a new trailer. "Experience their fight behind enemy lines in the form of three all-new bonus missions included in Halo: Campaign Evolved."

A summer release for the remake has long been rumoured, as Xbox - and everyone else in the industry - attempts to shuffle its line-up to steer as clear as possible away Rockstar's upcoming juggernaut, GTA 6.

Unlike most other upcoming releases in Xbox's catalogue, Campaign Evolved occupies the unique position of being the first Halo to ever be developed for Sony's PlayStation platform. From the beginning, the game had been envisioned as a multi-platform title.

As surprising as the news was at the time, it's worth keeping in mind that the decision was made in the Phil Spencer era of Xbox, which had all but shifted the brand's publishing focus to be almost entirely about multi-platform.

Newly-appointed CEO Asha Sharma, however, may be charting a different path. She has seemingly been keen - at least outwardly - to take the brand back into its early, platform-dependent days where exclusives were the norm. While that shift was unlikely to affect the initial plan for CE, it was still interesting to see final details confirmed today.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/halo-campaign-evolved-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day reportedly no longer coming to PlayStation 5
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE: PS5 release has been Fe-nixed, Xbox console exclusive release date confirmed.

UPDATE 6.09pm: And, just when I hit publish, Asha Sharma confirmed that Gears of War: E-Day will be an Xbox console exclusive. It arrives on 6th October, and will be available day one with Game Pass.

As well as this release date, we also have a new Gears of War: E-Day gameplay trailer. "Experience the horror and brutality of Emergence Day," reads the blurb. "Fourteen years before the original Gears of War, join Marcus Fenix and Dominic Santiago in a gripping origin-story campaign as the Locust Horde first erupts from below, igniting a desperate fight for survival."

The original story continues below.

ORIGINAL 6pm: It has been claimed that Gears of War: E-Day - which serves as a prequel to the original Gears of War, and is set 14 years before the events of that game - is no longer coming to PlayStation 5.

That's according to Jeff Grubb, who was talking during a recent Giant Bomb stream. "Gears of War: E-Day was going to be on PS5," Grubb said. "It's not anymore. That just happened. That is a decision that just got made." Grubb added that Xbox is "going backwards on this stuff".

Said Grubb: "They were like 'this battle doesn't make any sense', now they're like, 'exclusives!' To what, sell another 20m Xboxes?"

It was originally reported that Xbox Studio's Gears of War: E-Day would be coming to PlayStation 5 consoles last year. This was at a time when Xbox had been steadily adding more of its games to additional platforms. This included the likes of Grounded, Sea of Thieves and Hi-Fi Rush. Microsoft then went on to release the likes of Forza Horizon 5 and Indiana Jones and the Great Circle on PlayStation. Meanwhile, the original Gears of War was also released on PlayStation last year, as part of the revamped "Reloaded" edition of the game.

However, there have since been a number of high profile changes at Xbox, most notably Asha Sharma taking over from Phil Spencer as the brand's CEO in February. Sarah Bond also left Xbox at this time. Since her appointment, Sharma has been making a number of changes at Xbox, including cutting the price of Game Pass by taking Call of Duty out of the service.

Earlier this month, Sharma spoke about the company's stance on exclusivity for first-party games, stating she is aware that platforms "need" exclusive content, but also that successful publishers need to reach players everywhere they can.

At the time of writing, a PEGI listing for Gears of War: E-Day still lists PS5 as a release platform, so if indeed the game does end up skipping Sony's console, this was clearly a very recent decision.

A dedicated Gears of War stream is scheduled for later this evening (7th June), so we will hopefully have clarification one way or another on E-Day's release platforms. Before that, we also have the Xbox Games Showcase in a matter of minutes. Our Lottie is once again covering the roundup as the show happens, so be sure to stay tuned for all of the announcements and trailers to come.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/gears-of-war-e-day-release-platform-skipping-ps5-rumour
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Surprise! Cult hit Vampire Survivors is getting a new name, Vampire Survivors - First Survivaton, plus a new free expansion pack
item.content:encoded:
Expect 10 new characters, 16+ new weapons and evolutions, a new XL stage, eight new music tracks, and more.

Award-winning Vampire Survivors has announced a new expansion pack and a new name: Vampire Survivors - First Survivaton.

The surprising name change – dropped during Summer Game Fest week – comes as Poncle seeks to disaggregate its new lineup of "Survivors-like projects" separately from the studio's flagship title, hitherto known as Vampire Survivors.

"Survivaton is the name of Poncle's new lineup of Survivors-like projects," the team explained. "Short for 'survive a ton', the label encompasses internally developed games that build on the foundation of the studio's flagship title, Vampire Survivors, in collaboration with selected content partners. Each project will aim to introduce major gameplay changes, content expansions, or genre twists exploring ideas substantial enough to stand as their own games.

"The Survivaton initiative will serve as Poncle's umbrella for future survivors-like releases, and reflecting its place as the foundation of the series, Vampire Survivors will also be renamed to Vampire Survivors - First Survivaton."

To mark the change, Poncle has shadow-dropped its next patch, update 1.15, on Steam, introducing a raft of new content, with the patch coming to other platforms "a bit later, by the end of the month". The studio needs the additional time to manage "platform-specific optimisations and differences in save slots management".

"On the bright side, we will also be releasing a Nintendo Switch 2 version, with better performance and mouse support!"

While we don't yet have a firm release date for the DLC, Vampire Survivors: Legacy of the Bloodmoon Expansion is expected "this summer" and… Well. It's already June. You can expect 10 new characters, 16+ new weapons and evolutions, a new XL stage, eight new music tracks, and more. The studio is also expanding Legacy of the Moonspell with additional content and permanently reducing its price "ensuring both it and Legacy of the Bloodmoon deliver a similarly substantial experience".

"In ancient times, Saint Baal'Thasar had a vision of paradise, lit by a bright, red moon," Poncle explains. "And so he founded the Order of the Red Moon, to lead people to a land of eternal life. The myth says that Baal'Thasar found his paradise during a pilgrimage, and so never returned."

Vampire Survivors: Legacy of the Bloodmoon Expansion is coming this summer to PC, Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch, and mobile later this year.

Poncle has also expanded in Italy and Japan, with Sawaki Takeyasu overseeing the latter's local development.

Mentioned in this article

Vampire Survivors

Android, Mac, Nintendo Switch, PC, PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, iOS



https://www.eurogamer.net/vampire-survivors-new-name-first-survivaton-bloodmoon-dlc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Watch Xbox’s Biggest Summer Gaming Showcase In Years Right Right Here
item.content:encoded:
All eyes are on Xbox’s June Showcase, which is set to go live in less than an hour. That extra attention is partly because of the expected game reveals and deep dives into upcoming releases on the console platform’s 25th anniversary. But the other reason is to see how Microsoft decides to handle first-party exclusivity moving forward. Will Gears of War: E-Day be available on PlayStation 5 at some point? Or has the tech giant decided to yank that port at the last minute?

The latter is what Giant Bomb host Jeff Grubb claimed happened during last night’s Giant Bomb Couch livestream. Despite Gears being rated for PS5 and a Walmart placeholder listing even appearing online, the rumor now is that new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma may have decided to pivot last-minute to give the console’s most diehard fans a long-sought-after win.

You can watch the full Xbox 2026 summer showcase starting at 1:00 p.m. ET on June 7 below:

Exclusivity drama and first-party games aren’t the only things to look out for at the show. These press conferences also pride themselves on snagging exclusive reveals for big third-party games. One of those reveals that all of the leaks have been pointing to in recent weeks is Persona 6. Xbox has been getting a lot cozier with Sega, and Atlus’ next RPG is expected to appear during today’s showcase.

We’ll no doubt also get a lot of news about what new releases from the second half of this year will be coming to Game Pass, as well as updates on first-party games we haven’t heard from in a while. Those include stuff like State of Decay 3 from Undead Labs and, my personal most anticipated upcoming Xbox game, Clockwork Revolution from inXile Entertainment. The event will also be Sharma’s first time presenting Xbox on the Summer Game Fest circuit, and the first showcase in years that won’t be helmed by her predecessor, Phil Spencer.

Let us know what you’re hoping to see from the livestream and what you end up grading it in the comments below!



https://kotaku.com/watch-xboxs-biggest-summer-gaming-showcase-in-years-right-right-here-2000703604
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hot Wheels Infinite Rush Gameplay Reveals Huge Freely Explorable Islands | IGN Live 2026
item.content:encoded:
Hot Wheels Infinite Rush was revealed at Summer Game Fest on Friday and now we can exclusively reveal some extended gameplay from the upcoming racer. As you can see in the player above, Hot Wheels Infinite Rush is slated to include tons of new elements not found in its predecessors including a Drift Stunt Challenge, Stuntman Challenge, and Destroy Everything! Challenge, where players get to utterly wreck everything that possibly can within a set timeframe.

Ahead of the gameplay reveal at IGN Live 2026, I sat down with Michele Caletti, Development and Creative Director at Milestone - the studio behind the game, who told me that Hot Wheels Infinite Rush has a lot that fans of previous Hot Wheels Games will recognize, but marks a whole new world for the popular series.

“It’s not at all Hot Wheels Unleashed 3,” Caletta says. “It's a completely different thing.We wanted to keep some of the positive aspects that were very well recognized because basically Hot Wheels 1 and 2 are proper racing games with nuance, with content, with quality through and through. We built a community. So making Infinite Rush has been a very big challenge because we wanted to preserve the good, but we wanted to change a lot.”

Perhaps the biggest new element in Hot Wheels Infinite Rush is a series of freely explorable islands which allow players to hunt for a slew of discoverable items.

“There are going to be four big islands,” Caletti says. “You can freely explore, go around. [They are] different one from the other. You can find challenges, races, secrets, [and] collectibles. You don't select the next challenge or next race from a menu, but basically you go around, have a look and find things along the way in the classic open world pattern.

“This is a huge change because you can easily find yourself spending hours going around trying to catch the last secret, trying to find some new challenge that you've spotted. And it's completely different from the game loop perspective and from the way you perceive the game.”

In addition to the islands, Caletti says there’s a second major shift the development team implemented while building the game.

“The second huge change is the artistic vision placement,” Caletti says. “The other Hot Wheels games were based on the mantra of ‘Hot Wheels cars in real life-size, in toy form, in the real world, where you race in the back yard [or] in the kitchen.’ [With Hot Wheels Infinite Rush] we wanted to experiment with something very different.

“You'll still be a toy car, but you're going to race in a world made like a diorama. So there are islands themed like cities, like resorts. Everything is in the scale of the car yet it's somehow toy form. Everything is clearly made in plastic [so] there's a significant shift. The things we've been able to create from the artistic perspective is very, very different.

“On top, there are smaller changes,” Caletti says. “Here we have gone a step further in terms of differentiating the vehicles and creating four big classes with peculiar mechanics, their own vehicles, challenges, and race mechanics. Being able to zip around [the islands] opens up a different variety of challenges. Everything is somehow familiar and yet everything is different because of the scale factor.”

Beyond the new islands, mechanics, and scale that Hot Wheels Infinite Rush brings to the storied franchise, Caletti says that he’s excited for some of the more social elements the game will feature.

“I’d be very happy if [players] start interacting with each other online,” Caletti says. “Discussing how to catch all the secret items or the hidden items or the items that seem very hard to pick around the world. In other racing games, there's the discussion where to find all the cars, where to try to find all the secrets and to smash all the items. I'd like players to do the same with this game.”

For more IGN Live, check out all of the cool things you can see when the event wraps up later today.

Michael Peyton is the Senior Editorial Director of Events & Entertainment at IGN, leading entertainment content and coverage of tentpole events including IGN Live, San Diego Comic Con, gamescom, and IGN Fan Fest. He's spent 20 years working in the games and entertainment industry, and his adventures have taken him everywhere from the Oscars to Japan to Buenos Aires, Argentina. Follow him on Bluesky @MichaelPeyton

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/hot-wheels-infinite-rush-explorable-islands-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Games Showcase 2026 Live Updates: Every Game Announcement
item.content:encoded:
Every game, reveal, and surprise from Microsoft's summer showcase.

Xbox Games Showcase is back for another year, and this one promises a great deal from Microsoft. The showcase kicks off on Sunday, June 7 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm UK time, and we’ll have live updates below, bringing you all the announcements as they happen. It will be followed immediately by a Gears of War: E-Day Direct, which will deliver a deep dive on the next game in the action series.

So, what can we expect from Xbox Games Showcase itself? Microsoft has a number of big first-party games due out in what remains of 2026 and into 2027 that are still without release dates, so you’d expect those to be announced today. For example, Halo: Campaign Evolved, which we’re sure to get another look at during the show, will probably get a release date. A Gears of War E-Day release date is a sure bet, but will Microsoft also confirm a PS5 version? There’s been a lot of talk about Microsoft potentially returning to Xbox exclusives since new CEO Asha Sharma took over, so it will be interesting to see if any games at the showcase skip Sony’s console.

I’m also hoping for firm release dates for inXile Entertainment’s Clockwork Revolution, Undead Labs’ State of Decay 3, and Playground's recently delayed Fable. Meanwhile, we know we’ll get a look at the DMZ mode in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 during the show.

The 100 Best Xbox Games of All Time

Now, let’s speculate a little. We know Bethesda is working on a number of new games, but will any turn up at Xbox Games Showcase? How about that Fallout 3 Remaster? Could The Elder Scrolls 6 finally show off gameplay? Despite the fact TES6 was announced aeons ago, it showing up here somehow feels like wishful thinking.

One thing we know won’t be at Xbox Games Showcase is Project Helix, Microsoft’s next-generation console. This one’s all about the games, the company has confirmed. And, just like during Sony’s State of Play showcase, don’t expect GTA 6 to pop up at Xbox Games Showcase. Rockstar, as we all know, will do its own thing when it’s good and ready.

It’s worth noting that Xbox Games Showcase trailers will continue to display rival platforms, including PlayStation 5, but this may be the last time Microsoft does so. “Seeing the feedback on logos. It was a miss, and I own it,” Sharma said last month. “We are talking about how we adjust for future Xbox shows.”

Xbox Games Series Tier List

Xbox Games Series Tier List

While you wait to find out what Xbox has in store, let us know what you’re hoping to see in the comments below! And be sure to come back here in time for Xbox Games Showcase itself, and we’ll have everything announced as it happens.

Metro 2039

4A Games's Metro 2039 has a brand new trailer, showing off its gritty first-person shooting. It's due tou February 2027.

State of Decay 3

State of Decay 3 made its expected appearance at Xbox Games Showcase, bringing with it a new trailer. But Undead Labs' sequel still doesn't have a firm release date. All we got was a 2027 release window, which is a little disappointing.

Persona 4 Revival

Persona 4 Revival has a release date: February 18, 2027. And it's coming day one to Game Pass!

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy

Xbox Games Showcase brought us a new trailer for Asobo Studio's Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy. And yes, this one is coming day one to Xbox Game Pass.

Halo: Campaign Evolved

The new Halo: Campaign Evolved trailer teased the new story content coming to the game. Three new missions are included, complete with space combat. The release date is confirmed! July 28, 2026. Not long now.

Fable

Playground's Fable has a new trailer, confirming MCU star Hayley Atwell plays the main villain. We also have a release date: February 23, 2027.

Gears of War: E-Day

Xbox Games Showcase kicked off with a new trailer for Gears of War: E-Day, showing a young Marcus Fenix in civilian clothing fighting through the streets against the surprise Locust attack. And we have a release date: October 6, 2026. The big news here though is Gears of War: E-Day is Xbox console exclusive, which means there's no PlaySation 5 version. That's a massive call from new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma right there.

10 minutes to go! Last minute prediction time. What do you hope to see at Xbox Games Showcase? My fingers are crossed for something new from Fallout...

This is your 30 minute countdown! Xbox Games Showcase starts soon, and live updates will appear right here.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/xbox-games-showcase-2026-live-updates-every-game-announcement
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Smalland II: Lost Realms announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Maximum Entertainment has announced open-world survival crafting game prequel Smalland II: Lost Realms. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Taking place ages before the events of the first game, Smalland II: The Lost Realms brings you back down to size and into an expanded world filled with mythical creatures that are attuned to the natural elements. A world before the Giants colonized it, where you’ll discover new lands and cultures to help the Smallfolk thrive.

The elders foresee a tragic omen that will devastate the lands—can you prevent catastrophe from happening?

  • ❮
  • ❯

Key Features

  • Explore Mysterious Lands – Traverse a wonderous world filled with peril, mystery, and magic, where you will discover an array of lost realms, each with their own unique cultures and resources.
  • Survive Together – Play alongside friends with support for up to 10 in multiplayer. Explore together, fight together, build together and survive as a team.
  • Form Companionships – As marvelous and dangerous as the world is, you are not alone. Tame nature by forming a party of creatures that can use their distinctive abilities to help you in your journey. Bond and take care of these animals and they will take care of you.
  • Connect with Others – Discover and bond with the world’s inhabitants to gain access to new resources, technologies and arcane secrets.
  • Build and Link the Lands – Scavenge or refine resources to build outposts throughout the world and establish trade routes between the various lands.
  • Craft Powerful Items – Collect and craft armor and equipment to survive the perilous world. Glide through the skies, traverse the branches, navigate the waters and more.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/smalland-ii-lost-realms-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Son Of Thanjai Is An Action Game Set In 11th-Century South India Coming To PS5, Xbox, And PC
item.content:encoded:
Son of Thanjai is an open-world action game set in 11th-Century South India, a unique setting in the medium, and if that pitch doesn't sell you on it, its gameplay trailer might. That's because it looks awesome, with the princely protagonist Vinnendhira Chola utilizing the Indian whip-sword weapon known as a Surul Vaal to dismember enemies invading his one-day kingdom in a violently bloody fashion. 

At 19 years old, Vinnendhira has spent his life behind palace walls, avoiding responsibility and enjoying royal comfort. But his father is dying, and enemies are preparing to overthrow the family throne, so Vinnendhira is thrown into an adventure where he must fight tooth and nail (and whip) to survive. Son of Thanjai also takes an alternate-history approach to the Chola Empire, alluding to surprises likely in store when the game launches sometime in the future on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC. 

Check it out in the Son of Thanjai gameplay reveal trailer below: 

Looks pretty cool, right? There aren't many games set in India, and even fewer (maybe none) set in 11th-Century South India. This setting and the game's premise, coupled with this first look at gameplay, have caught my attention, and I look forward to checking it out one day. 

Here are some more details from the Steam page: 

"Cast out, Vinnendhiran travels a kingdom at the height of its power and on the edge of collapse: temple crowns crowded with merchants and pilgrims, fertile river plains, dangerous frontier roads, occupied settlements, and courts thick with ambition. Some still believe in the Cholas. Others have suffered because of them. Most are simply trying to survive. The farther he travels from the throne he lost, the more he learns how little he ever understood the people he was born to rule, and how much he will need them."

There's no word on when Son of Thanjai will launch, but the Steam page says "coming soon," so hopefully we can check it out on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC in the near future. 

For more, check out the coolest games we've played during the Summer Game Fest media play days, and then catch up on our Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase round-up for the latest announcements, updates, news, and more. 

What do you think of this gameplay reveal? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/07/son-of-thanjai-is-an-action-game-set-in-11th-century-south-india-coming-to-ps5
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Barbie and Godzilla aren’t scared of GTA6
item.content:encoded:
The biggest video games have been running scared from Grand Theft Auto VI’s release window so it’s up to two legends to take a stand. In 2023 it was Barbenheimer, for 2026 it’s Barbzilla!

Launching on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC via Steam on 3rd November 2026 is Godzilla: Destroy All Monsters Melee Remastered, a remake of the surprisingly fun Godzilla: Destroy All Monsters from 2002.

It is essentially a beat ’em up featuring Godzilla and his kaiju sized pals whacking seven shades out of each other. I vaguely remember playing this on PS2 and it was lots of smashy fun.

Key Features

  • The original 2002 Godzilla arena fighter, remastered.
  • 12 Kaiju to choose from, each with a uniquely vicious moveset.
  • Eight locations, including recreated real-world cities, Monster Island, and the Alien Mothership.
  • Gallery items to unlock.
  • Play with friends in local cooperative play and all-new online multiplayer.
  • Remastered graphics enhance the cinematic experience of the original.
  • Improved unlock system gives you the choice to unlock monsters, locations, and gallery items in any order.
  • Also includes additional single-player campaigns for each kaiju.

A week later the somewhat smaller,  but no less mighty, Barbie enters the fray with Barbie Rewind, a collection of 16 classic Barbie games from 1991 through 2007, including the never-before-released game, Barbie: Vacation Adventure.

As Barbie can do anything the games are quite varied and include horse riding, snowboarding, modelling, and that time when Barbie became a secret agent and could become invisible.

The games includes:

  • Barbie
  • Barbie Game Girl
  • Barbie Super Model
  • Barbie Vacation Adventure
  • Barbie: Race & Ride
  • Barbie Super Sports
  • Barbie: Pet Rescue
  • Barbie: Groovy Games
  • Super Agent Barbie: Royal Jewel Mission
  • Barbie Horse Adventures Blue Ribbon Race
  • The Barbie Diaries: High School Mystery

Barbie rewind is coming to PS5 and Nintendo Switch, and there’s a collectors edition for Switch players with an exclusive Barbie doll.

Source: Press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/barbie-and-godzilla-arent-scared-of-gta6/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: XBOX Games Showcase 2026—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
Xbox and Microsoft could be set for one of their most pivotal presentations in years. The XBOX Games Showcase 2026 could be a huge sign of things to come for the green brand, so don’t miss a single reveal or trailer.

In the blink of an eye, Summer Game Fest 2026 is coming to an end. Already, we’ve been treated to a plethora of stellar spectacles: The Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase, the Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase, and The MIX.

It’s XBOX‘s turn.

Everything Announced During XBOX Games Showcase 2026

Bad Magpie

Fable

Gears of War: E-Day

Halo: Campaign Evolved

Join Us

Metro 2039

Persona 4 Revival

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy

Sea of Thieves Update

State of Decay 3

Wo Long 2: Wings of Ember

Let us know which game you’re most excited for and what you hope to see from the XBOX Games Showcase 2026. You can do this and talk about it live with our community via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved Preorders To Go Live Today

Gears of War: E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved’s preorders will go live right after today’s showcase. Here are more details.

Gears of War: E-Day PS5 Version Debate Sparks Online

There’s some confusion about Gears of War: E-Day’s PS5 release, with some claiming it won’t happen and others suggesting it might.

Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG No Longer Moving Forward

Paramount Games Studio executive comments on game projects no longer in development related to its IPs.

Little Nightmares 3 ‘The Backstage’ DLC Coming Next Week

The Backstage will soon be available in Little Nightmares 3. The new DLC expansion drops next week.



https://insider-gaming.com/xbox-games-showcase-2026-everything-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dogpile coming to Switch 2, Switch, iOS, and Android; major update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

Roguelite deckbuilder currently available for PC.

Publisher WINGS, and developers Studio Folly, Toot Games, and Foot have released a major update for roguelite deckbuilder Dogpile, as well as announced that the game is coming soon to Switch 2, Switch, iOS, and Android.

Dogpile first launched or PC via Steam on December 10, 2025.

The major content expansion adds 13 new dogs, two new traits (zoomy and stinky), seven new languages, and a new challenge mode.

Here is an overview of the game, via Wings:

About

Dogpile is a roguelike deckbuilder about merging cute dogs into bigger dogs. Play dogs, get money, customize your dogs with special Traits, refine your deck, PLAY MORE DOGS! DOGS!

Key Features

  • Merge Up a Storm
    • When two of the same dog touch, BAM! You get a BIGGER dog. With careful placement, some clever purchases, and a can-do attitude, you can merge pups strategically and achieve the biggest dogs imaginable.
    • You really shouldn’t adopt a dozen dogs, but now you can! Dogpile gets you to wrangle a cute collection of canines into the perfect deck, customize them, and play them expertly. Manage the clutter, upgrade and improve your cards as you go, and strive for the BIGGEST dogs.
  • Customize Your Dogs – Like in real life, dogs come with quirks and untapped potential! You’ll discover any variety of weird traits such as Barky, Sleepy, Timid, Cool and more. Take your dogs to the Dog Wash to add even more traits, and find Dog Tags that bolster and benefit from Trait synergy!
  • Game Breaking Combos – Utilize a clever array of powerful Dog Tags to modify how your dogs and their Traits function. Find interesting synergies and unique combos to aid you in all of your merging endeavors. And always remember your ultimate goal: BIGGER!

Watch a new trailer below.

Major Update and New Platforms Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/dogpile-coming-to-switch-2-switch-ios-and-android-major-update-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Leaf Blower Co. coming to PS5, Xbox Series this fall
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Xbox Series

Currently available for PC.

Publisher Forklift Interactive and developer LifT Games will release Leaf blowing simulation game Leaf Blower Co. for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series this fall, the companies announced.

Leaf Blower Co. first launched for PC via Steam on November 17, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via Forklift Interactive:

About

“Pile up the leaves, then pile up your cash…”

Blow your leaves and your cares away in this chilled out work sim. Build up your business, buy new tools, and unlock upgrades – all while watching the leaves fly. After all, one man’s trash is another man’s cash.

Key Features

  • Tidy Up Your Own – Play through the full story of Leaf Blower Co., clear away clutter and bring back a world of peace and tranquility. And of course, no-one expects you to do all of this for free! Spend your hard-earned cash on new gear, upgrades, and even new styles. It’s important to have the right tool for the job, and if you don’t do it, who else is gonna skin your bin?
  • A Job for All Seasons – Leaf blowing is not all just sunshine and rainbows—after all, the school playground still needs those leaves gone even if it is pouring with rain; a sudden snow storm at the tennis court is just an excuse for you to really put your leaf blower through its paces; and while it’s a bit unusual that the Mayor needs a last minute clean up of his garden after sunset, who are you to say no?
  • Channel That Inner Peace… – Feel the true satisfaction of restoring peace and tranquility after a job well done; get in the zone as you blow away anything out of place; and lose yourself as you wander through a host of warm and welcoming locales.
  • …And Hunt Down Every Secret – Each location has a selection of optional bonus objectives and hidden Easter eggs throughout to reward exploration of every nook and cranny. It’ll take a keen eye and knowledge of all the tools at your disposal to find them all!
  • Blow It Your Your Way – When you’ve finished the Campaign, look no further than Free Play mode to satisfy that Leaf Blowing itch. With the ability to choose whatever location takes your fancy, your perfect weather conditions, and your ideal time of day, the choice is completely up to you. From a snowy morning on the beach to a glorious sunset in the park, and everything in between, play Leaf Blower Co. your way!

Watch a new trailer below.

Consoles Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/leaf-blower-co-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-this-fall
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
